## Overview

AI-driven automation framework for WeChat mini-games on macOS. A universal `GameLoop` (capture в†’ analyze в†’ execute) drives per-game adapters. Currently supports `endless_winter` (ж— е°Ѕе†¬ж—Ґ).

## Commands

```bash
# Setup
python3 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
pip3 install -r requirements.txt
brew install cliclick          # REQUIRED вЂ” see "macOS input" below

# Run (game is dynamically loaded from games/<game>/game.py)
python3 main.py --game endless_winter
python3 main.py --game endless_winter --dry-run           # analyze only, no clicks
python3 main.py --game endless_winter --max-rounds 1 --log-level DEBUG

# Backend selection
python3 main.py --use-gemini                                         # Gemini cloud (needs GEMINI_API_KEY in .env)
python3 main.py --local-api-url http://192.168.1.156:1234            # local Qwen VL (default)

# Debug
tail -f debug/autoplay.log
ls debug/screenshots/   # every round's screenshot + AI response is saved here
```

There is no test suite, no linter, no build step. Validate changes by running against the live game in `--dry-run` mode first.

## Architecture

### Core flow (core/game_loop.py)
Single loop: screen capture via Quartz в†’ optional scene classifier / change detector в†’ `game.analyze_with_retry()` (or fallback `build_prompt` + `ai_client.analyze`) в†’ `parse_ai_response()` в†’ `execute_action()`. The loop also handles game-over detection, restart, and consecutive-failure bail-out. **Modifications to per-game behavior should almost always live in the game adapter, not in `GameLoop`.**

### Game adapter pattern (core/base_game.py)
`main.py` dynamically imports `games.<name>.game` and instantiates `<CamelCase>Game(config_dict, ai_client=...)`. Every game subclasses `BaseGame` and must implement `build_prompt`, `parse_ai_response`, `execute_action`. Optional hooks: `is_game_over`, `on_game_over`, `on_round_start`, `on_round_end`, `identify_scene`, `analyze_with_retry`. `GameLoop` reads `game.top_offset`, `game.bottom_offset`, `game.resize_width`, `game.use_resize` directly off the instance вЂ” set these in `__init__` from config.

### endless_winter dual-system
Open-world tap game with a **dual-system** architecture (see `games/endless_winter/game.py`). System 1 = fast local Qwen VL for UI element detection; System 2 = Gemini cloud fallback (enabled by presence of `GEMINI_API_KEY`) gated by `system2.min_interval_s`. Also runs a `SceneClassifier`, `ChangeDetector` (MSE-based NO_CHANGE gate), `StuckMonitor` (A-B cycle + same-zone repeat detection), stuck-zone blacklist, and stale-screenshot validation. Do not simplify these without understanding the failure modes they guard against вЂ” most are direct responses to documented bugs.

### Advanced capabilities (all toggleable in `config/games/endless_winter.yaml`)
- **`planner`** (`core/planner.py` + `config/plans/endless_winter.yaml`): DFS task-tree picks the first pending leaf; its `active_task_context()` (description + expected_scene + success_keywords) is injected as the first line of every S1 prompt. Task completion uses **stability** (N consecutive idle rounds in `expected_scene`, configured per leaf via `stability_rounds`) rather than keyword matching вЂ” keyword hits were too noisy. Leaves that recur (popups, task bar) set `repeat_until_stable: true` so `mark_success` immediately re-arms them to `STATUS_PENDING`.
- **`completion_checks`** (`core/completion_checks.py`): optional pluggable check registry. A task can declare `completion_check: qwen_yesno` (or a dict with `type` + kwargs); `Planner.tick(ctx, idle)` dispatches to the registered callable first and only falls back to stability if it returns False. `qwen_yesno` is currently a stub that raises `NotImplementedError` вЂ” caught and logged at debug level, so scaffolded yaml entries don't break runtime. Designed for a future "ask the VLM if the task is done" hook.
- **`memory`** (`core/memory_store.py`, SQLite `data/memory.db`): cross-session trajectories, per-(scene,target) skills that stabilise after 2+ successes, and persistent TTL blacklist (checked in `_do_click`).
- **`verifier`** (`core/verifier.py`): records expected_scene before acting; after the next frame compares `(scene_after, changed)` to drive `planner.mark_failure`, `memory.record_action`, and `memory.add_blacklist` on failures. Success is now driven by planner stability, not by verifier outcomes.
- **`learning`** (`core/trajectory_logger.py`): writes one JSONL row per infer+action+verify to `data/trajectories/`. `scripts/export_dpo.py` pairs same-scene success/failure responses into DPO `{prompt, chosen, rejected}`.
- **`system1.model_family`** (`core/model_router.py`): `qwen_vl` (default) | `ui_tars` | `cogagent`. Non-qwen families go through `_run_system1_routed` and are evaluated by `scripts/run_benchmark.py` against `tests/benchmark/scenarios.yaml`.
- **`research`** (`core/research.py` + `scripts/fetch_research.py` + `data/research/<game>.md`): Gemini-fetched Chinese guide snippets, cached with markdown frontmatter (`fetched_at`, `refresh_days` staleness check). When enabled, `load_for_prompt(prompt_budget_chars)` returns в‰¤600 chars that are prepended as `гЂђжёёж€Џж”»з•ҐеЏ‚иЂѓгЂ‘` to every S1 prompt (ahead of the planner task context). **Defaults to `enabled: false`** вЂ” `data/research/endless_winter.md` ships as a cold-start placeholder with hand-written 4X hints; run `python3 scripts/fetch_research.py --game endless_winter` to replace it.

Turning any of these off is a one-line yaml change; turning them all off restores the pure dual-system behaviour.

### Config
Global defaults in `config/settings.yaml`; per-game overrides in `config/games/<name>.yaml` loaded via pydantic (`utils/config_loader.py`). `.env` holds `GEMINI_API_KEY` and is loaded at startup. Never hardcode coordinates or timing вЂ” add a config field.

## Non-obvious constraints

- **macOS input**: route all clicks through `cliclick` (already wired in `core/input_controller.py`). `pyautogui` events are silently dropped by WeChat вЂ” they appear to succeed but register nothing in the app. Also call `ScreenCapture.activate_wechat()` to make WeChat frontmost before clicking.
- **`vision.top_offset` is per-game and affects click math**: captured window is cropped from the top by `top_offset` pixels, and click Y is `win_y + top_offset + y`. `endless_winter` uses 40 (88 crops away the in-game в†ђ / вњ• buttons). If the model reports "ж‰ѕдёЌе€°иї”е›ћ/е…ій—­жЊ‰й’®" when they're clearly on screen, suspect `top_offset` first.
- **Qwen VL coordinate format**: bbox `(xmin,ymin,xmax,ymax)` normalized to 0вЂ“1000, always. Never ask Qwen for pixel coords. Always require a text description of the target *before* the bbox вЂ” coord-only prompts hallucinate.
- **Screenshot skip after action**: `game_loop.skip_next_screenshot` intentionally drops one frame after a successful action so the game can render. Don't "fix" it.
- **Image resize for cost**: `vision.resize_width` controls pre-AI downscale. Currently set to 720 in **cost-saving mode** (testing phase, cloud-only); restore to `null` if Qwen bbox accuracy regresses. Paired with `inference.image_quality: 50` (was 70) and `change_detection.threshold: 25` (was 40, more aggressive NO_CHANGE gating).
- **`memory.skill_min_success`**: threshold to promote a `(scene,target)` coord to a reusable skill. Currently `1` вЂ” a single verified success is enough. `verifier` + TTL blacklist act as safety net if the cached coord later misfires.

## Memory system

Project-local `memory/` has been removed. Hard-won lessons live only in Claude's auto-memory dir (`/Users/pillar/.config/claude/projects/-Users-pillar-Documents-workspace-wechat-game-automation-agent/memory/`), auto-loaded in future sessions. When persisting a new lesson, write there and update its `MEMORY.md` вЂ” do not recreate a project-local `memory/`.
